herbs.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, has been used for centuries to create a soothing poultice to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. The poultice is typically made by crushing the leaves and flowers of the plant, then mixing them with a carrier oil or water to create a paste. When applied directly to the skin, the poultice is said to promote healing, reduce pain, and prevent infection in minor wounds and injuries.
